q: One word substitution: 'A person who speaks many languages'.
e: A Polyglot is someone who can speak or write in several languages fluently.

q: Select the Synonym of the word: 'METICULOUS'.
e: Meticulous means showing great attention to detail; being very careful and precise.

q:
Cloze Test: Select the most appropriate option for Blank No. 1.

'Health is the (1)______ wealth of a person. If we (2)______ our health, we lose everything. A healthy person (3)______ enjoy life to the fullest. Regular exercise (4)______ us fit and active. Therefore, we should (5)______ healthy habits.'
e: Blank 1: 'Ultimate' is the correct choice as health is considered the greatest/final wealth.

q:
Cloze Test: Select the most appropriate option for Blank No. 2.

'Health is the (1)______ wealth of a person. If we (2)______ our health, we lose everything. A healthy person (3)______ enjoy life to the fullest. Regular exercise (4)______ us fit and active. Therefore, we should (5)______ healthy habits.'
e: Blank 2: 'Neglect' fits the condition 'If we... we lose everything'.

q:
Cloze Test: Select the most appropriate option for Blank No. 3.

'Health is the (1)______ wealth of a person. If we (2)______ our health, we lose everything. A healthy person (3)______ enjoy life to the fullest. Regular exercise (4)______ us fit and active. Therefore, we should (5)______ healthy habits.'
e: Blank 3: 'Can' expresses the ability of a healthy person to enjoy life.

q:
Error Spotting: Select the part with the error.

Neither the captain (A)/ nor the players (B)/ is responsible for the defeat. (C)/ No Error (D)
e: Rule: When subjects are joined by 'Neither-nor', the verb must agree with the nearest subject. Since 'players' is plural, use 'are' instead of 'is'.

q:
Error Spotting: Select the part with the error.

He is (A)/ senior than (B)/ me in the office. (C)/ No Error (D)
e: Rule: Words ending in '-ior' (senior, junior, superior, inferior) are followed by the preposition 'to', not 'than'.

q:
Error Spotting: Select the part with the error.

If I was you (A)/ I would not (B)/ accept this offer. (C)/ No Error (D)
e: Rule: In imaginary/hypothetical sentences (Subjunctive mood), we always use 'were' regardless of the subject. Correct: 'If I were you'.

q:
Error Spotting: Select the part with the error.

Scarcely had he (A)/ gone out (B)/ than it started raining. (C)/ No Error (D)
e: Rule: 'Scarcely' and 'Hardly' are always followed by the conjunction 'when' or 'before', never 'than' (which is used with 'No sooner').

q:
Error Spotting: Select the part with the error.

The furniture (A)/ in this room (B)/ are very old. (C)/ No Error (D)
e: Rule: 'Furniture' is an uncountable noun and is always used in the singular form. Therefore, it takes a singular verb 'is'.

q:
Direct-Indirect: Select the correct indirect form.

He said to me, "I am writing a letter now."
o: He told me that he is writing a letter now.
He told me that he was writing a letter then.
He said me that he was writing a letter then.
He told me that I was writing a letter then.
e: Rule: 'Said to' becomes 'Told'. 'Am writing' (Present Continuous) changes to 'Was writing' (Past Continuous), and 'now' changes to 'then'.

q:
Direct-Indirect: Select the correct indirect form.

The teacher said, "The Sun rises in the East."
o: The teacher said that the Sun rose in the East.
The teacher told that the Sun rises in the East.
The teacher said that the Sun rises in the East.
The teacher said the Sun will rise in the East.
e: Rule: If the direct speech is a Universal Truth or Habitual Fact, the tense does not change even if the reporting verb is in the past tense.

q:
Direct-Indirect: Select the correct indirect form.

She said, "Where are you going?"
o: She asked where I was going.
She asked where was I going.
She said where I was going.
She asked that where I was going.
e: Rule: In Interrogative sentences, 'said' changes to 'asked'. The sentence becomes assertive (Subject + Verb), and 'that' is not used with 'Wh' questions.

q:
Active-Passive: Select the correct passive voice.

The chef prepares a delicious meal.
o: A delicious meal was prepared by the chef.
A delicious meal is prepared by the chef.
A delicious meal is being prepared by the chef.
A delicious meal has been prepared by the chef.
e: Rule: For Present Indefinite (V1), use 'is/am/are + V3' in passive voice. 'Prepares' becomes 'is prepared'.

q:
Active-Passive: Select the correct passive voice.

They are painting the wall.
o: The wall is painted by them.
The wall was being painted by them.
The wall is being painted by them.
The wall has been painted by them.
e: Rule: For Present Continuous (is/am/are + ing), use 'is/am/are + being + V3' in passive voice.

q:
Active-Passive: Select the correct passive voice.

Shut the door.
e: Rule: For Imperative sentences (Order/Request), the passive structure is usually 'Let + Object + be + V3'.
